codeReview.summary: Implementation creates pattern_learning.rs with RejectionData, AntiPattern, Convention structs, PatternLearningAnalyzer, and PatternLearningService. Tests pass and build succeeds. However, there is a critical bug where MemoryEntry.repository is set to String::new() instead of self.repository when creating new convention blocks.

issues.description: BUG: repository field is set to String::new() when creating a new MemoryEntry in update_memory_blocks(). This means all convention memory blocks created will have an empty repository field, causing get_by_label queries (which filter by repository) to fail to find them. Should be repository: self.repository.clone()

Magic numbers hardcoded in PromotionThresholds::default(). The values (pattern_min_confirmations: 5, pattern_min_confidence: 0.6, etc.) match settings.json but AGENTS.md says 'Never hardcode magic numbers - always load from .swell/settings.json'. However, no crates currently load settings.json, so this is a systemic issue.

Semantic issue: deduplicate_anti_patterns passes frequency_map.values().sum() as total_rejections to update_confidence(). This sums all pattern frequencies across ALL patterns, not the actual number of rejections. For example, if 5 rejections each produce 2 patterns, sum would be 10. While this may produce reasonable results, the variable name and semantics are misleading.

Feature gap: PatternLearningAnalyzer::analyze returns anti_patterns but they are never persisted - only conventions are stored via update_memory_blocks(). The get_anti_patterns_for_error method always returns empty (marked TODO in code).

Parameter store is unused in PatternLearningAnalyzer::new (marked _store). The analyzer doesn't persist anti-patterns - it only returns them in memory. The store parameter suggests future persistence capability but is not connected.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md says 'Never hardcode magic numbers - always load from .swell/settings.json', but no crates currently load settings.json. The pattern_learning module hardcodes PromotionThresholds that match settings.json values (confidence_promotion_threshold: 0.6, pattern_promotion_confirmations: 5, etc.). This is a systemic gap - either the guideline should be implemented or relaxed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: PromotionThresholds::default() hardcodes values that exist in .swell/settings.json under memory. No crates use serde_json::from_reader or similar to load settings.

summary: The pattern_learning module provides pattern extraction from rejection feedback with good test coverage. Build passes and 14 tests pass. However, there is a CRITICAL bug at line 1087 where MemoryEntry.repository is set to String::new() instead of self.repository, causing repository-scoped queries to fail. Additionally, anti-patterns are returned but never persisted, and the store parameter in PatternLearningAnalyzer is unused.
